originalTitle: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
title: Sad News from a School: Let's Learn About Safety and Being Kind
content:
Hello, Wonderful Children of Africa!

Hello, my dear friends! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I'm here to share some news with you. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad. But even when it's sad, we can learn important things about how to keep ourselves and our friends safe. Today, we are going to talk about something that happened in a school called Utumishi Girls' School in a country called Kenya. Kenya is a beautiful country in Africa, just like many of your homes.

A Fire at School

Imagine your school. You go there to learn, to play, and to see your friends. Many schools in Africa have places where children can sleep, especially if their homes are far away. These places are called dormitories. **Did you know?** A dormitory is like a big bedroom where many children sleep together at school. It's a place where they rest after a long day of learning and playing. Sadly, last month, something very, very scary happened at Utumishi Girls' School. There was a fire. A fire is when something burns very quickly, and it can be very, very hot and dangerous. This fire started in one of the dormitories, where the girls were sleeping. It made everyone very sad and scared.

Sixteen Girls Lost

When the fire happened, sixteen girls lost their lives. These girls were students, just like you. They were between 15 and 18 years old. That's a bit older than you, but they were still young and full of dreams. It is very, very sad when we lose someone, especially children. Our hearts are with their families and their friends who miss them very much. **Did you know?** When there is a big fire, grown-ups called firefighters come to help put it out. They are very brave and wear special clothes to keep them safe from the heat.

Why Did This Happen? Grown-ups Are Looking for Answers

When something very bad happens, like a fire, grown-ups always try to find out why. They want to know what happened so they can try to stop it from happening again. They ask questions and look for clues, just like detectives in a storybook. People called investigators are working very hard in Kenya to understand how the fire started at Utumishi Girls' School. They want to know the truth.

Sad News About Some Students

Now, here is the part of the news that is even more difficult to hear. The grown-ups who are investigating have found out something very serious. They think that some of the older students from the same school might have done something very, very wrong that caused the fire. They are saying that these students might be charged with something called 'murder'. **What does 'charged with murder' mean?** It means the grown-ups who are in charge of the law believe that these students did something that led to the death of the sixteen girls. It means they think it wasn't an accident. It is a very serious thing to say that someone caused harm on purpose. The law grown-ups will listen to everyone and find out exactly what happened. This process is called a 'trial'. **Did you know?** In every country, there are rules called laws. These laws help us live together safely and fairly. If someone breaks a law, especially a very serious one, grown-ups have to talk about it and decide what is right.

Why is it Important to Be Kind and Safe?

This sad news reminds us how important it is to be kind to each other. Sometimes, people get angry or upset, and they might make bad choices. But bad choices can hurt many people. It is always, always better to talk about your problems with a trusted grown-up – like your parents, your teachers, or your guardians. They are there to help you. **Did you know?** Fire needs three things to start: fuel (like wood or paper), heat (like a match or a stove), and oxygen (the air we breathe). If you take one away, the fire cannot start or will go out!

Learning About Fire Safety

This sad story also teaches us about being very careful with fire. Fire can be helpful – it cooks our food and keeps us warm. But it can also be very dangerous. Here are some very important rules to remember about fire safety: * **Never, ever play with matches or lighters.** These are tools for grown-ups only, and they can start a fire very quickly. * **If you see a fire, tell a grown-up immediately!** Don't try to put it out by yourself. Grown-ups know what to do. * **Know your way out.** If there is a fire at your school or home, grown-ups should teach you the safest way to get outside. * **Stay low to the ground.** If there's smoke, the air is clearer closer to the floor. Crawl to safety! **Did you know?** Many schools have special practices called 'fire drills'. During a fire drill, everyone pretends there is a fire and practices leaving the building quickly and safely. It's important to listen to your teachers during these drills!

Let's Look Out for Each Other

Even though this news is very sad, it reminds us to be careful, to be kind, and to always, always talk to grown-ups when we have worries or see something wrong. Our schools should always be safe places for learning and growing. Let's all promise to look out for our friends, listen to our teachers, and make good choices every day. We can help make sure that bad things like this don't happen again. Thank you for listening, my dear friends. Remember to stay safe and be kind to one another. Your grown-ups love you and want to keep you safe.
summary: Sixteen girls sadly died in a fire at their school dormitory in Kenya last month, and grown-ups are now investigating and believe some older students might have caused the fire on purpose, reminding us all to be kind and safe.
whyMatters: This news matters because it teaches us how important it is to be safe from fires, to always tell a grown-up if we have worries or see something dangerous, and to always be kind to our friends so everyone can feel safe at school.

originalTitle: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
title: Helping Our Friends in Sudan Stay Safe
content:
Hello my amazing friends! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter from right here in Africa. Today, we're going to talk about a country called Sudan. It's a really big and beautiful country, just like many others in our wonderful continent of Africa. Can you imagine a place with big rivers and lots of interesting animals? That's Sudan! But sometimes, even in beautiful places, grown-ups have big, big problems.

What's Happening in Sudan?

Right now, some grown-ups in Sudan are having a very big disagreement. It's like a really, really serious argument between two teams of people. When grown-ups fight like this, it can make things very difficult and scary for everyone, especially for children and families who just want to live peacefully and play. This fighting has been going on for a little while, and it has made many people very sad and worried. Imagine if your neighbours were always shouting and pushing each other; it would make you feel unsafe, wouldn't it? Well, in Sudan, it's a bit like that, but much bigger and more serious. People are sometimes forced to leave their homes, their schools, and even their toys behind because it's not safe to stay. This is called 'conflict,' and it's when different groups of people can't agree and start to fight. Did you know? Sudan is one of the biggest countries in Africa! It has a long, long history and amazing pyramids, just like Egypt.

Feeling Safe is Super Important

Every single person in the world, especially every child, deserves to feel safe. Feeling safe means you can play, go to school, eat your food, and sleep without being scared. It means no one should hurt you or make you feel bad. But sometimes, during these big grown-up fights, some very, very bad things happen that make people feel super unsafe and scared. The United Nations, which is like a big team of grown-ups from all over the world, has been watching what is happening in Sudan. They have seen that some grown-ups are doing mean and hurtful things to people, especially women and girls. These hurtful things make people feel very, very afraid and upset. It's never okay for anyone to hurt another person or make them feel unsafe. Did you know? The United Nations is like a big family of almost all the countries in the world. Their main job is to help people, keep peace, and make sure everyone is treated fairly.

What is a 'Weapon of War' in this Story?

Now, when we hear 'weapon of war,' we might think of big guns or tanks, right? But the United Nations is saying something very important here. They say that some grown-ups are using mean and scary actions, like hurting people's feelings and bodies, as a 'weapon.' This means they are using these very bad actions, not just guns, to scare whole families and communities so much that they leave their homes. It's like if a bully at school tried to scare you away from your favourite playground by doing really mean things, not by using a stick, but by saying hurtful words or pushing you around in a very scary way. In Sudan, these very mean and hurtful actions are happening to grown-up women and girls, and it makes them feel terribly unsafe and scared. It is never their fault that these bad things happen to them. It is always the fault of the people doing the hurting. These bad actions are aimed at making people lose hope and feel so frightened that they pack up everything and run away. This is not how anyone should be treated. It's a very sad thing when people use fear and hurt to get what they want.

The United Nations is Speaking Out!

The good news is that the United Nations is speaking very loudly about these sad things. They are saying, "Stop! This is not okay!" They want everyone to know that these hurtful actions are happening, and they want them to stop right away. The UN helps by telling the world what is happening so that more good people and countries can come together to help. They want to make sure that the people doing these bad things are stopped and that everyone in Sudan can feel safe again. The UN also tries to help families who have been hurt or scared by giving them food, water, a safe place to sleep, and grown-ups who can listen to their worries and help them heal their hearts and bodies. Did you know? The UN has special helpers called peacekeepers who wear blue helmets and work to keep people safe in places where there is fighting.

Hope for a Safer Future

Even though these things sound very sad, it's important to remember that there are many, many good people in the world. The United Nations and many other helpers are working super hard to bring peace and safety back to Sudan. They want all children and grown-ups in Sudan to be able to play, learn, and live without fear again. We must always remember to be kind to each other and to speak up if we see or hear anything that makes us or our friends feel unsafe. If you ever feel scared or worried, always tell a trusted grown-up, like your parents, your teacher, or another family member. They are there to help you and keep you safe. Let's all send our kindest thoughts and hopes for peace and safety to our friends, brothers, and sisters in Sudan. We hope that soon, everyone there can live happy, safe lives, full of laughter and joy, just like all children deserve. Thank you for listening, my dear friends. Until next time, remember to be kind and stay safe!
summary: The United Nations says that some grown-ups in Sudan are using mean and hurtful actions, not just guns, to scare people and make them leave their homes, and many good helpers are working to make things safe again for everyone there.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone, especially children, deserves to feel safe and live without fear, and many helpers are working hard to stop the bad things happening in Sudan so people can live in peace.

content: England fans have been left frustrated by the goalless draw against Ghana, but were Thomas Tuchel's side fortunate not to have given away a penalty?

originalTitle: US judge blocks Trump subpoenas into transgender care at New York hospitals
title: A Judge Says 'No!' to Peeking at Private Health Papers!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here! Today, we have a very important and kind story from far, far away in a country called America. It’s a story about being fair, keeping secrets safe, and making sure everyone feels happy and respected!

What Happened Far Away?

Imagine you have a very special book, like a diary, where you write down your secret thoughts or draw secret pictures. You wouldn't want anyone to just open it and read it without asking you, right? Those secrets are yours!

Well, in America, something a little bit like that almost happened. A big group of grown-ups, who worked with a man named Mr. Trump, wanted to look at some very special secret papers. These papers were about people who visit doctors and nurses at big buildings called hospitals.

But then, another very important grown-up, who is called a 'judge', stepped in! The judge said a big, clear: "NO! You cannot look at those private secret papers!" It was like a superhero stopping something unfair from happening, making sure everyone's privacy was protected!

What Are These 'Secret Papers'?

When you go to the doctor, or when your mum or dad takes you for a check-up, the doctor writes down important things about your health. For example, if you had a cough, or if you needed a bandage for a scrape. These notes are called 'medical records'.

These medical records are super private. They are like your own special treasure box of health information. Doctors promise to keep them safe and not tell anyone else without your permission. This is called 'confidentiality', and it's a very important rule!

Did you know? Keeping your health information private helps you trust your doctor. It means you can tell them anything that helps you feel better, and you know they will keep it safe and help you safely!

Who Wanted to Look at the Papers?

The team that wanted to look at these private papers was called the 'Trump administration'. Think of them like a big team of grown-ups who help run the country of America. Mr. Trump was in charge of this team when this happened.

They wanted to do something called a 'probe'. A probe is like when someone wants to look very, very closely at something, sometimes to find out if rules are being followed, or just to learn more. But this time, it was about very personal and private health details, which are usually kept secret.

What Is 'Transgender Care'?

This is a bit of a grown-up word, but let's make it simple. Sometimes, a person might feel like they are a boy on the inside, but their body looks like a girl's on the outside. Or maybe they feel like a girl inside, but their body looks like a boy outside.

These people are called 'transgender'. It just means their inside feeling about themselves doesn't quite match what people might see on the outside. And 'care' means doctors and other kind helpers make sure they are healthy and happy, helping them feel comfortable and be their true selves. It's all about being kind, understanding, and supportive, so everyone can live happily.

Did you know? Everyone is special and unique! Some people like to wear bright colours, some like quiet games. Being yourself is one of the best and bravest things you can be!

Who Said 'NO!'? The Judge!

So, a very important grown-up called a 'judge' stepped in. A judge is like a super smart referee for grown-ups and for the law. Their job is to make sure everyone follows the rules fairly and that things are fair for absolutely everyone in the country.

This judge carefully looked at what Mr. Trump's team wanted to do. And the judge decided it was not fair or right to peek at such private health secrets. The judge said it could hurt people's feelings, make them scared, and stop them from wanting to go to the doctor when they needed help.

Did you know? Judges often work in special buildings called courthouses. They usually wear special robes, like a uniform, to show everyone that they are doing a very important and serious job of upholding justice!

Why Was It Important for the Judge to Say 'No'?

The judge's job was to protect people and make sure everyone is treated with respect. It's like when your teacher makes sure everyone in the classroom is treated kindly and no one is left out or picked on. Everyone deserves to feel safe!

By saying 'no', the judge helped keep people's private health stories safe and sound. It also helped make sure that everyone, including transgender people, can feel safe and brave enough to get help from doctors without worrying that their private secrets will be looked at by others without their permission.

It's all about fairness, respect, and kindness. Everyone deserves to be treated with dignity and respect, no matter who they are or how they feel inside their heart and mind.

Did you know? In many places around the world, it's a very strong rule that doctors must keep your health information a secret. This important rule helps build trust between doctors and their patients!

What Are Hospitals For?

These private papers were kept at 'hospitals' in a very big city called New York. Hospitals are very important and amazing places where doctors, nurses, and other kind helpers work very hard to make sick people better and help people stay healthy.

When you're not feeling well, or if you have an accident, hospitals are there to give you medicine, help you heal, and make sure you grow up strong and healthy. They are places of care, healing, and hope for everyone!

Did you know? The very first hospitals were often started by kind people who simply wanted to help others who were sick, injured, and had nowhere else safe to go for help!

What Does This Mean for People?

This news means that people in America, especially those who are transgender, can feel a bit safer and more comfortable. They know that their private health information is still private and protected, just as it should be.

It means they can keep trusting their doctors and go to the hospital or clinic when they need help, knowing their secrets are safe. It’s a happy ending where fairness, kindness, and privacy won the day!

Keep Being Kind!

So, my dear friends, remember this wonderful story. It teaches us that being fair, being kind, and respecting everyone’s private space and feelings is super, super important. We should always try our best to make sure everyone feels safe, happy, and understood, just like the kind judge did!

That’s all for today from your African news reporter! Stay curious, keep learning, and keep being kind to everyone around you!
summary: A special judge in America stopped a big grown-up team from looking at private health papers about people who need help feeling comfortable being themselves, making sure everyone's secrets stay safe and fair.
whyMatters: This is important because it means doctors can keep your health secrets safe, and everyone, no matter who they are or how they feel, can get help without worrying that their private information will be shared without their permission. It's all about being fair, kind, and respecting everyone's privacy and feelings.

originalTitle: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
title: Africa Kids News: France Has a Super Hot Day!
content:
Hello from Your Africa Kids News Reporter!

Hello, amazing young friends across Africa! It's me, your friendly kids' news reporter. I have some exciting (and a little bit warm!) news from far, far away, all the way in a country called France. France is in Europe, a continent across the big ocean from us. Even though it's far, what happens there can teach us important things about our world. Today, we are talking about a very, very hot day!

What is Happening in France?

Imagine the sun shining super bright, making everything feel like a giant oven! Well, that’s a bit like what happened in France recently. They had a day that was incredibly hot. So hot, in fact, that it was the hottest day they have ever, ever measured! Can you believe it? The sun was really showing off its power! This kind of super hot weather is sometimes called a 'heatwave'. It's when it stays very hot for many days, not just one. It can make everyone feel very sticky and tired. Even the ground feels warm under your feet. People have to find ways to cool down, just like we do when the sun is really strong here in Africa.

Feeling Super Hot!

How hot was it, you ask? Well, it was like nothing they had seen before! The temperature was so high that it broke an old record. A record is like the best score in a game. So, this was the highest temperature score ever! The country was sweltering, which means it was uncomfortably hot and humid. Think about the hottest day you have ever felt. Maybe you wanted to jump into a pool, or stand under a cool shower, or just sit in the shade with a nice cold drink. That's exactly how people in France felt. They were trying their best to keep cool and comfortable, just like we would. Did you know? We measure how hot or cold it is using something called a thermometer! It has a little red line that goes up when it's hot and down when it's cold. Scientists use super special thermometers to get exact measurements.

How Do We Know It's the Hottest?

How do we know it was the hottest day ever? Well, clever scientists and weather people have been keeping an eye on the weather for a very, very long time. They write down the temperature every single day. They have been doing this since a long time ago, even before your parents were born, way back in 1947! When they checked all their notes and measurements, they saw that this recent day was hotter than any other day in all those years. It means that the numbers on their thermometers went higher than they ever had before. It's like finding a new biggest fruit in the garden! It's a record! Did you know? The earth's temperature has been measured for hundreds of years, but special tools for weather, like the ones in France, started in the last 100 years or so! This helps us compare how hot or cold it is over time.

Oh No, No Electricity!

Now, when it gets so, so hot, something else can happen. Sometimes, the lights might go out! This is called a 'power outage' or a 'blackout'. It means the electricity stops working for a little while. This happened in some parts of France because it was so hot. Why does this happen? Well, when it's super hot, lots of people want to turn on their fans or air conditioners to cool down. These machines use a lot of electricity! Sometimes, when too many people use too much electricity all at once, the power lines and power stations get tired. They can’t handle all that work, and so the electricity might stop working to keep everything safe. Imagine many, many people all wanting a drink from one small water tap at the same time. The tap might struggle to give water to everyone! It's a bit like that with electricity. It’s important to have electricity for lights, refrigerators (to keep food fresh!), and many other things. Did you know? Power outages can also happen during big storms or when lightning strikes power lines. So, it's not just heat that can make the lights go out!

Staying Safe When It's Hot

When the weather is super hot, it's very important to stay safe. People in France, just like people here in Africa, know some good tricks to keep cool. What do you think they do? * They drink lots and lots of water! This is super important to stop your body from getting too hot and tired. * They try to stay in the shade. Sitting under a big tree or inside a cool building helps a lot. * They wear light clothes, like thin cotton shirts, that let their skin breathe. * They might even take cool baths or showers to bring their body temperature down. * And sometimes, they just slow down and rest. It’s too hot for running around a lot! These are all smart ways to deal with hot weather. It's good to remember these tips for when it gets very sunny where you are too! Did you know? Some animals, like dogs, pant (breathe fast with their mouths open) to cool themselves down because they can't sweat all over their bodies like humans do!

Why This News Matters to Everyone

So, why is this news from far-off France important for us, here in Africa? Because our Earth is one big home for all of us! What happens in one part of the world can sometimes tell us something about what might happen in other parts. Scientists around the world are watching these super hot days. They want to understand why these heatwaves are happening more often and getting hotter. It helps us all think about how we can take better care of our planet. When the Earth gets too warm, it affects everyone and everything – the weather, the plants, the animals, and even us!

Let's Look After Our Amazing Earth!

So, my young friends, this news from France reminds us that our world is an amazing place, but it also needs our care. We can all do little things to help keep our Earth healthy. Maybe it’s turning off lights when we leave a room, or not wasting water. Every little bit helps! Stay curious, stay cool, and keep learning about our incredible world! That's all for today from your Africa Kids News Reporter!
summary: France recently had its hottest day ever recorded since 1947, leading to power outages in some areas, and teaching everyone about staying safe in very hot weather.
whyMatters: This news from France is important because it shows how our planet is getting warmer, affecting weather and electricity, and it reminds us all to take care of the Earth and stay safe when it's super hot.

originalTitle: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
content: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.
